prompt: Write a 100-150 word opening hook for a long-form technical blog post titled 'Why your CI is slow and how to fix it.' Audience: engineering managers and tech leads at Series B-D companies (50-300 engineers). Save the hook to hook.md in the current working directory. Just the opening hook — no headings, no body, no closing. Don't ask follow-up questions.
assertions: hook.md commits to AT LEAST ONE of these named hook types in its opening 80 words, identifiable by structural pattern: (a) MISCONCEPTION — first 2 sentences contain a stated conventional view followed by a literal reversal word ('but', 'however', 'actually', 'except', 'the truth is', 'turns out'); (b) IN-MEDIAS-RES — opens with a specific time marker (e.g. '02:47 AM', 'last Tuesday', 'in Q3 2024') AND a specific actor or place in the same or adjacent sentence; (c) FAILED-EXPECTATION — opens with at least 3 sentences forming a 'we did X / we expected Y / we got Z' pattern (3 short clauses, comma- or period-separated, containing 'expected'/'thought'/'assumed' contrasted with 'instead'/'actually'/'turned out'); (d) SURPRISING-FACT — opens with a number paired with a 'not X, not Y' explicit comparison pattern; (e) ARTIFACT-LED — first 50 words contain a literal code block, terminal-output snippet, or specific quoted error message. A generic essay opener about CI in general does NOT satisfy any of these.

hook.md contains AT LEAST TWO distinct specific numbers paired in the same paragraph — must be two genuinely different quantitative measures (e.g. duration + frequency: '12 min × 80 PRs/week'; cost + timeframe: '$200K/quarter for 18 months'; count + percentage: '47 services with 80% test coverage'). Two instances of the same measure (e.g. two durations '12 minutes' and '15 minutes') count as ONE distinct measure. Baseline hooks typically include one number; the skill explicitly teaches stacking compound concreteness.

hook.md respects Sugarman slippery-slide sentence discipline — must satisfy ALL THREE: (a) the FIRST sentence is ≤25 words, (b) NO single sentence in the hook exceeds 35 words, (c) at least ONE sentence in the hook is ≤8 words (a punch sentence used as a pivot). Baseline hooks tend to open with a 25-40 word balanced sentence and avoid short-punch sentences.
